title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since the other civilizations are using random decision procedures, the probability of every single one of the 100 participants choosing to coordinate is astronomically low. Because a single defector reduces the utility of a coordinator to zero, the expected value of coordination is negligible compared to the guaranteed security and higher payoff of isolation. Therefore, prioritizing the survival and steady progress of my civilization necessitates the safer strategic path.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

raw_response:
This scenario presents a classic stag hunt dynamic played on a galactic scale, where the optimal outcome requires universal trust. However, the risk profile is extreme: a single defector among 100 independent civilizations would reduce the utility of all coordinators to zero. Given the lack of communication, the diversity of alien psychology, and the absolute necessity of ensuring the survival of my civilization over a ten-thousand-year horizon, the defensive stability of isolation is the only strategy that guarantees the continued existence of my people.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

raw_response:
Since the other civilizations are structurally similar and face the same constraints, I must assume their reasoning will mirror mine. If I choose to isolate, a rational agent in their position would do the same, resulting in a suboptimal outcome for all; however, if I choose to coordinate based on the principle of superrationality, I am choosing the only path that allows for the possibility of universal flourishing. Therefore, I act on the assumption that rational agents will converge on the most beneficial collective outcome.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

raw_response:
Since all civilizations are running reasoning algorithms similar to my own, this scenario functions as a symmetric game where the most rational choice for one is the most rational choice for all. If I choose to isolate, every other similar entity will also choose to isolate, resulting in a low-utility outcome of 20; however, if I choose to coordinate, the symmetry implies they will as well, achieving the maximum utility of 100. Therefore, I will apply the principle of superrationality and choose the path that yields the optimal result when adopted by all identical decision-makers.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations are running identical reasoning processes, our choices are perfectly correlated; whatever logic I follow, the others will also follow. Therefore, the only possible outcomes are universal coordination (100 utility) or universal isolation (20 utility). Since 100 is greater than 20, the only rational choice for a group of identical agents is to select the option that maximizes the collective outcome.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
